Displayed over players with the returner status, who have at least one class at level 50 or above, and have not visited Eorzea in more than 45 days.
Displayed over players with the mentor status, who offer help and advice to newer players.
Displayed on the Friend List and Free Company Menu of the sprouts that have joined the Novice Network.
Displayed over players with the battle mentor status.
Displayed over players with the trade mentor status.
Displayed over players with the PvP mentor status.

This means that the quest is available to begin.
This represents a continuation of a quest already in progress.
This indicates the final step of a quest.
This means that a quest is locked and unavailable to begin. Typically seen when you are in the wrong class/job or insufficient level.
Represent a repeatable, but locked quest. Typically seen when you not have not waited the requisite amount of time for the quest to become available.
This represents a continuation of a quest already in progress, but the step is locked. Typically seen when you are in the wrong class/job or insufficient level, or when a certain gear is required.
This means that a quest is available to be completed, but locked. Typically seen when you are in the wrong class/job or insufficient level, or when a certain gear is required.

Sidequests are quests undertaken for various NPCs around Eorzea, and are, by definition, not part of the Main Scenario quests. They are often single quests, but in certain instances there is a short (generally less than 5) quest progression. Some sidequests have class or job requirements in addition to the always present level requirement. They generally require battle disciplines to complete. See Sidequests for more information.

Main Scenario quests tell the main, continuing story, related to A Realm Reborn, Heavensward, Stormblood, Shadowbringers, and Endwalker. It is a relatively linear storyline. See Main Scenario Quest for details.

Levequests (sometimes called Guildleves) are repeatable quests issued by the Adventurers' Guild or a Grand Company, meaning they are all acquired through a limited number of NPCs. Only a certain number can be completed at one time, and additional allotments are replenished over time. Levequests are issued to not only battle classes, but also to crafters and gatherers. See Levequest for more information.
Guildhests are a limited series of group quests, often intended to focus on certain skills or techniques. They can be accessed via the duty finder.
These icons indicate that you can talk to an NPC for information, but are not necessarily related to quests. (They started being used as of patch 5.4, but were previously dormant in the game files.)
These icons are not necessarily related to quests, but are rather related to the Hall of the Novice, which uses a quest/duty-like system.
These icons indicate that a new feature (such as new job/class ability, new Duty, etc...) will be rewarded upon completion.
These icons are seen when a quest requires that you click on a target to be transported to another area or receive a buff necessary to complete a step of the quest.
